text: Good evening! Let's take 5 minutes to process how today went.
Looking back, how would you describe the overall 'vibe' of your day?
options: Productive & Smooth|Challenging but Managed|Frustrating & Out of Control|Slow & Passive
answer=Productive & Smooth|Challenging but Managed:A1_Q_VICTOR;answer=Frustrating & Out of Control|Slow & Passive:A1_Q_VICTIM

text: You felt in flow. When things went well, what was your role in it?
options: I prepared and executed well|I stayed focused despite distractions|The stars aligned for me|I just did my job

text: A tough day. When things felt out of control, what was your first thought?
options: Why is this happening to me?|Who is responsible for this mess?|What is the one thing I can still control?|I'll just wait for tomorrow

text: That's an internal locus. You're recognizing that your choices shape your outcomes. Keep that agency high.
It's easy to feel like a passenger on hard days. But remember, even in chaos, you choose your response.
Now, let’s shift from how you handled the day to what you brought to the table for others.
Think about your interactions today. Which of these resonates most?
options: I went out of my way to help a colleague|I shared my knowledge to solve a problem|I felt I wasn't given enough support|I focused strictly on my own tasks
answer=I went out of my way to help a colleague|I shared my knowledge to solve a problem:A2_Q_CONTRIB;answer=I felt I wasn't given enough support|I focused strictly on my own tasks:A2_Q_ENTITLE

text: Giving energy feels good. What motivated you to contribute?
options: The team's success is my success|I enjoy solving problems|It's my responsibility|I wanted recognition

text: It's okay to feel overlooked sometimes. What did you feel was missing today?
options: Proper appreciation from management|Clearer instructions from others|Help from lazy teammates|Nothing, I just stayed in my lane

text: You're operating with a contribution mindset. This is how leaders are built—by adding value beyond the job description.
Entitlement often masks frustration. Try to find one small thing you can 'give' tomorrow without expecting a return.
Finally, let's look at the 'size' of your world today.
When you were stressed today, what was the primary concern in your head?
options: My workload and my deadlines|My reputation and how I look|How my stress affects the team|The value we are (or aren't) delivering to the customer
answer=My workload and my deadlines|My reputation and how I look:A3_Q_SELF;answer=How my stress affects the team|The value we are (or aren't) delivering to the customer:A3_Q_ALTRO

text: It's natural to focus inward when under pressure. How did that feel?
options: Exhausting and lonely|Necessary for survival|I didn't think about it|I felt in control

text: You kept the 'Big Picture' in mind. Why was that important today?
options: Because we're all in this together|It makes my own problems feel smaller|It's the only way to get real results|I want to be a helpful person

text: A narrow radius can make problems feel huge. Tomorrow, try to zoom out.
Self-transcendence is a superpower. By focusing on the 'Us', you've contextualized your own challenges.
Reflection Complete. Today you showed {axis1.dominant} agency, a focus on {axis2.dominant}, and a {axis3.dominant} radius of concern.
Rest well. You've done the hard work of thinking. See you tomorrow!
